author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! nivedana śunaha āmāra
janmile maraṇa āche, kāla-dūta āche piche,
bhuñjāibe karma⋅anusāre

jāvata āchaye āi, ‘kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa’ kaha bhāi,
kahi ‘kṛṣṇa’ sāra’ āpanāke
kṛṣṇa-nāma je vadane, se jitila tribhuvane,
ki bhaya śamane āche tāke

jadi cinta nija⋅hita, sādhu⋅saṅge kara prīta,
asatsaṅga nā kariha kṣaṇe
kukkura⋅bhavane gele, asthi⋅carma khuba mile,
gaja⋅danta⋅muktā siŋha⋅sthāne

kṛṣṇa⋅nāma⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅, śravaṇa⋅kīrtana, mana,
aśru⋅kampa⋅pulaka ānande
sādhu-saṅge sadā vasi’, vilasaha divāniśi,
tabe vāñchā pure premānande
en_translation:
O my mind! Please listen to my humble appeal. If you are born, you must die, and the messengers of death are always behind you. They will make you experience the results according to your past actions.

As long as you still have life, chant "Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa!" my brother. By chanting the Name, protect your soul. One on whose lips the Name of Kṛṣṇa resides has already conquered the three worlds; what fear can he have of the God of Death?

If you desire your own welfare, then develop affection for the company of the saintly. Do not associate with the unholy for even a single moment. If you go to a dog's house, you will find only bones and skin, but in a lion's place, you will find elephant tusks and pearls.

O my mind! With tears, trembling, and ecstatic joy, always hear and chant the Name, pastimes, and qualities of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Always sitting in the association of the saintly, enjoy these pastimes day and night; then your desires will be fulfilled in divine bliss.
